article_title: Xbox 'Reset' Hits Ninja Theory Days After Its Showcase Reveal as Microsoft Braces Studios for Cuts Past Fiscal Year-End
summary: Substantively accurate and well-sourced; one Dom Matthews quote silently elides 'as a character' without an ellipsis — filed as a correction.

findings.message: A direct Dom Matthews quote is shortened mid-sentence without an ellipsis.
findings.details: Article quotes: "It's a bold new step for Senua into the action-adventure space." The Xbox Wire source (source-4) reads: "It's a bold new step for Senua, as a character, into the action-adventure space." The article dropped "as a character" inside quotation marks without marking the elision. Meaning is preserved; flagged via corrections record.

editor_notes.content_quality: Clear, well-structured News piece with disciplined sourcing and an appropriate 'reported/claimed' framing. Separates confirmed memo facts from the unconfirmed studio-closure reports, and includes a 'What We Don't Know' section noting Microsoft has not officially confirmed shutdowns.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 5 snapshots read from disk (status 200, no archive fallback). source-0 (Game Developer): confirms Sharma + Matt Booty memo, 'optimism and realism', 'next 100 days', '$20 billion on content, platform, and hardware investments over the past five years', 'annual revenue decline by almost $500 million', 'over 5x the prices we paid only two years earlier' re console storage. source-1 (Northeastern Global News): confirms Celia Pearce as 'game design professor at Northeastern University', both Pearce quotes verbatim, and the $20B / nearly $500M figures independently. source-2 (VGC main): confirms spin-off negotiations citing Bloomberg's Jason Schreier, 'buy themselves back from Microsoft and go independent...', 'informed of the situation... still in flux' quote, Sharma 'resetting'/'not in a healthy spot', fiscal year June 30, South of Midnight (Compulsion), Kiln (Double Fine), Ninja Theory/Hellblade/Senua. source-3 (VGC, Game File): confirms 'sunset or split with the studio' and 'draw investor interest' quotes attributed to Game File 'citing a source familiar with Microsoft's operations'. source-4 (Xbox Wire): confirms Matthews quotes, Unreal 5, 2027 release, platforms (Series X|S, PC, cloud, Steam, PlayStation 5). All allowlisted.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Every figure, name, date, and event traces to a cited allowlisted source. Studio spin-off/closure correctly framed as reported (not Microsoft-confirmed). No bot-blocked-only specifics remain ('nine days' / 'British studio' framing absent). One quote elision noted below.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional; no sensationalism or AI self-reference.
editor_notes.originality: New topic. No prior Machine Herald coverage of the Xbox reset / Ninja Theory closure reports. Cross-reference links to Phil Spencer retirement, Game Pass pricing, and Double Fine unionization all resolve to existing articles.
concerns: One direct Dom Matthews quote drops the phrase 'as a character' mid-sentence without an ellipsis. Meaning is preserved but the quotation is not verbatim; filed as a public correction.
recommendations: When trimming inside quotation marks, mark the elision with an ellipsis to keep direct quotes verbatim.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Accurate, neutral, well-sourced News piece. The sole issue — a single quote silently shortened by three words without an ellipsis — is honestly summarizable in a corrections note, so APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS rather than REJECT.

content_preview.title: Xbox 'Reset' Hits Ninja Theory Days After Its Showcase Reveal as Microsoft Braces Studios for Cuts Past Fiscal Year-End
content_preview.summary: Reports say Microsoft is closing Hellblade studio Ninja Theory and put Double Fine and Compulsion in exit talks, days after a public Xbox 'reset' memo.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Microsoft's gaming division has begun what its leadership calls a business "reset," and several of its own studios are now reported to be facing closure. According to [Video Games Chronicle](https://www.videogameschronicle.com/news/xbox-studios-compulsion-double-fine-and-ninja-theory-facing-closure-its-claimed/), citing Bloomberg's Jason Schreier, three Xbox-owned studios — Compulsion Games, Double Fine, and Ninja Theory, the studio behind the *Hellblade* series — are negotiating to...
